message:
perf(process): replace spin/backoff poll loop with kernel blocking wait

Child::wait() previously used a user-space polling loop that alternated
between spin_loop()/yield_now() (up to 32 iterations) and 1ms sleeps,
calling try_wait() repeatedly until the child exited. This wasted CPU
on spin iterations and added unnecessary latency from the 1ms sleep
backoff for processes that exit quickly.

Replace with a direct call to the underlying std::process::Child::wait(),
which uses the kernel's blocking waitpid(2) syscall. The child handle is
consumed (set to None) after a successful wait, preserving the existing
"child already waited" error semantics. This eliminates all user-space
polling overhead while maintaining identical API behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
